nativeRange.habitat: Shallow reef environments, typically found on hard substrates in the photic zone

decorPreferences: Attach to live rock via wedging, zip-tie/fishing line, or superglue
Do not bury in sand — red algae will melt at the base if buried

avoidWith: strong herbivorous fish that may consume macroalgae

imageCandidates.notes: Peter Crowcroft, 2048x1152. Live tidepool specimen of Coelarthrum opuntia (type species of the genus) held in hand; sharp, vivid red fluid-filled bladder clusters clearly visible against green algae — best diagnostic view of the genus.
Peter Crowcroft, 2048x1152. Same species/session, different angle; crisp red branching bladder structures of Coelarthrum opuntia with tidepool background.
Peter Crowcroft, 2048x1152. Alternate frame of Coelarthrum opuntia; sharp close view of the red club-shaped bladders.

summary: String-of-Pearls is an ornamental red alga (Coelarthrum sp.) notable for its clusters of round, fluid-filled bladders that resemble a bunch of grapes. It grows slowly and is typically kept attached to live rock in reef display tanks.
careNotes:
String-of-Pearls is a red macroalga in the family Rhodymeniaceae, recognizable by its rounded, fluid-filled bladders. The structure is similar to Botryocladia but lacks the pronounced branching stem found in that genus. It is generally considered an intermediate-level display species for reef aquariums.

Attachment: secure fragments to live rock by wedging them into crevices, tying loosely with zip-tie or fishing line — take care not to crush the thallus (the branching body of the alga) — or using superglue gel on a rock plug for short-term hold.

Lighting should be full-spectrum; actinic-only spectra, the blue-purple wavelengths often used to make corals fluoresce, are generally inadequate on their own. Flow should be gentle to moderate, similar to what is used in a soft-coral display tank; excessive flow has been reported to tear delicate branches.

Weekly dosing of an all-around micronutrient supplement covering iron, iodine, and trace elements is generally advised. Target calcium at 400–450 ppm and magnesium at 1250–1350 ppm to maintain a stable reef environment. Nitrate in the range of 5–20 ppm and phosphate at 0.02–0.1 ppm provide adequate nutrients for growth.

**Do not bury the base in sand** — red algae in this family tend to deteriorate at the holdfast (the anchoring base) if submerged in a sand bed. Fragments may arrive with small hitchhiking organisms; inspect on receipt and remove anything unwanted before introducing the alga to an established tank.
breedingNotes: Propagation in captivity is primarily by fragmentation: breaking off a healthy branch segment and re-attaching it to live rock using one of the methods described above. String-of-Pearls also undergoes an alternation of generations in the wild — cycling between a spore-producing phase and a gamete-producing phase — but this part of its life cycle is not typically managed or observed in aquarium settings.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Macroalgae Care Guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source. Provided water parameter targets (temp 22–26°C, pH 8.1–8.4, Ca 400–450 ppm, Mg 1250–1350 ppm, NO3 5–20 ppm, PO4 0.02–0.1 ppm), lighting guidance (full-spectrum), flow guidance, and the sand-bed melt warning for red algae. Cross-verified as the authoritative secondary for Mosaic-only entries per playbook.
